reviews.review_title: Great art & design, some movement issues
reviews.review_description:
As someone who enjoys the Dutch art, there is a lot to like about this app. Compared to other art and museum apps, it is the best and most detailed and well-rendered display of art that I have seen. The lighting and the resolution are great, and really helps you enjoy the art and feel like you're almost there. I would certainly love to see more from this developer. However, I really wish there was free movement instead of teleportation.  I often ended up in kind of weird spots in front of the painting because I couldn't quite get the angle and perspective that I wanted, much easier with the free walking.See the Stadel Time Machine app for a great example of what it can feel like to walk through a museum. Also, when I re-center with my controller on the Gear VR, it often automatically rotates me. A fairly annoying issue.

 While the setting was cool and the environment graphics are solid, I actually would have appreciated something that feels more like a museum so it felt more like a travel experience. But it doesn't detract from the art, definitely recommend this app.The Kremer guy seems cool, wish I could be friends with him in real life.

developer_response.body:
Dear Ted, thanks so much for this wonderful review! Here are some answers to the comments you make:
Oculus Home - We actually have a selection of our paintings to the Oculus home items earlier this year, but so far Oculus has been having trouble to make it work. I've contacted them again to alert them we really want to share these with you! Hopefully, they will show up in your inventory soon!

Movement - on the OGO/Gear VR, the only way to move is by teleporting as the headsets have no tracking. On other headsets with tracking,  you are free to move around and roam the museum as you wish...This goes for our new iOS and Android apps too ;)

Rotation - we'll look into this!

The Kremer Guy - I assume you mean my father in the hologram: nehh...he's ok ;)

Thanks again! Best regards, Joel Kremer

reviews.review_description: After a few minutes you basically get an idea of what this experience offers. The pros include - amazing scale of the building, easy navigation and wonderful artwork. The cons are - most of the frames around the artwork suffers from aliasing and “shimmer” which detracts somewhat from the paintings. The voice overs pretty good but not great - and the ambient music doesn’t have a volume slider. And on the topic of volume the info narration gets louder the closer you get to a painting - which while that seems cool can be a little jarring if you’re not expecting it. Overall it’s a good experience right up there with Anne Frank House or Mars is a Real Place - the only thing holding it back from 5 stars are the cons I mentioned and a lack of variety in art styles. Maybe add DLC for sculptures that you can rotate or other styles of paintings.

developer_response.body: Dear JWB, thank you for your honest feedback; always valuable! We re continuously trying to improve the experience based on user feedback and hardware updates (i.e. aliasing/shimmer)and will take your comments into account. With regards to art styles; this collection is a collection of dutch old masters from the 17th century; we'd love to build more and different collection experiences and are talking to a number of private collections and institutions....but the artworld moves (very) slow... ;) Stay tuned...Cheers, Joel Kremer

reviews.review_description: the museum is very nice but we Italian users, when we can listen to the description of the works even in Italian language?

developer_response.body: Dear Jackmav, thank you for your feedback. We will put Italian as a language on our roadmap to do list! Kind regards, Joel Kremer

reviews.review_description: The experience is very good but please do something for the controls : Trigger is much better to use than the surface control of the GO and make the possibility to turn around in a seated position and zoom in on the pictures.

developer_response.body: Dear LANFEUST, thanks for the compliment! We've chosen the swipe pad for a number of reasons, one being that we wanted to make the experience the same for all platforms we are on (vive, daydream, oculus, WMR) so have kept this the same for  the OGO. Funnily enough, when we submitted our first build, we did have the trigger enabled, but the content team at Oculus advised us to have only 1 option. We will continue to revisit our UX with every update tho and I have put this through to our dev teams. Thanks again and enjoy the museum! Joel Kremer

reviews.review_description:
Very impressive!  
Interface is smooth, clean, intuitive .. feels like the future has arrived. 
Looking forward the possibilty of experiencing many more museums, galleries, & collections with this technology. 
Addition of gift shop, cafe (immersive environment to casually enjoy views, music, etc & perhaps socialize) may add to the experience.

A few years ago I had a chance to visit the Netherlands and see some of the stunning art. What really stands out in this experience is it feels just like that visit, but from the comfort of home. From designing the space via architect, to having the pictures actual scale to see them, to the remarkable lighting - this is the best I’ve seen. My only two recommendations would be to skip the initial scene (it is more confusing than helpful) and add in swipe to rotate (as most Go users are on their couch). I’d love an automated tour as well, even if it just walked you through seeing the art. But really exceptional app!
The app is not working , each time we enter the space it chrashes after half a minute

developer_response.body: Thank you for reporting this. I'm sorry that you had to experience this. We've (just now) fixed the issue with the museum intro video. There is a new version in the store that doesn't crash and gives you a proper greeting when you enter the museum. Feel free to reach out when you have other issues or ideas to improve the experience.

reviews.review_description: Beautiful! Love it. As close to a physical museum experience as you can get. Though there is no way that any museum guard in the real world will you let walk or jump through a painting to have a look at the back, in this museum you can :). Very entertaining and lots of info.
I returned the app. If several people complain about the controls and the dev promises to take care of the criticism but then fail to deliver I have no problem to return the product, pity.

developer_response.body:
Hi VonDragan, 
updated: we have released our rotation controls and floorplan! Hope you like it! Cheers, Joel

we're sorry you feel this way: we are finalizing our rotation controls, as we try and do things platform agnostic, which in our case we need to find a solution for the Go, the Rift, the Vive, Vive Focus, Daydream etc. We've done that and will release this update in the next few weeks. We hope you try this update and let us know what you think. Obviously, any additional feedback is welcome! Cheers, Joel Kremer

reviews.review_description: Great museum experience!  Lots of paintings from various artists, and presentation is beautiful and high definition.  The whole experience feels professional and top notch!

Very well done. Perfect. Just like being in a museum minus travel and crowds.
Please add more content.
Thanks

developer_response.body: Thank you very much! We will be adding more content and features going forward, but any suggestions are welcome! Cheers, Joel Kremer

reviews.review_description: This is great.  I bought this a few months ago, I go in and explore from time to time.  It relaxes me.   I feel like I am actually in a museum only better, no crowds and no sore feet!  Looking forward to more pieces!

I’m not a giant art fan myself, but this was like going to a real museum and the audio (and little bit of video for one piece) made me check out everything and I did enjoy it. Takes a hour or two to finish and is worth it I think.

This is an awesome virtual reality experience  for any art lover . First of all it is user very friendly and as an architectural student , i first fell in love with the design of the Museum which i admired for a while before going on to view the paintings . This got me smiling and i felt more thrilled when i found the info option at the bottom of each painting that explains your currently viewed art , and its essence .
To me , this idea from the makers of the Museum doesn't just let you view the beautifully made art, but puts you in a state which makes art inevitable.

Taking off the headset would give you the feeling of returning from a well built art Museum and i believe this would impact greatly in schools and art lovers around the world , saving you the costly travel and bringing the museum experience to you wherever you may be. I think this is an amazing experience and one would tell that so much hard work has been done to perfect this experience.

This is the best virtual reality Museum in the World and on behalf of me and  friends in NIgeria, that have tried out these app we say more grease to your Elbows Kremer and all that made this experience a success .

This app is a wonderful way to experience some great art and should be the template for all museum/art gallery apps. There has been an update recently which has vastly improved the controls, so now moving about is very easy, and you can get really close to the art whilst hearing a brief audio file about the picture you are viewing. The visual quality is superb, and feels like you are really there. Highly recommended to art lovers.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review Richard!

reviews.review_description:
The images of the paintings are high quality, and the additional information and narration for the pieces is a nice touch. The environment is well-designed and offers the feeling of viewing pieces in a real museum.

I recommend using the app in an environment in which you have full freedom to rotate 360º. I tried the app for the first time while sitting in bed and the controls weren't suited well for this. Movement is by teleport only. There were several times I wished I could adjust or rotate my position a few inches using the touchpad, as those types of fine adjustments felt cumbersome using the teleport method.

Overall, it's a quality app for art lovers, and I wish there were more experiences like this available.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback Jay! We are looking into your suggestions and will update the app every so often to incorporate user feedback. Given this is not the first time we've heard this feedback, we will put it o the roadmap! Kind regards, Joel Kremer
